result.markdown:
--- title: "Hands-on Exercise 4c: Visualising Uncertainty" author: "carina peh" date: "May 6, 2026" date-modified: "last-modified" execute: eval: true echo: true warning: false freeze: true --- ## Overview Visualising uncertainty is about showing not just the point estimate (like a mean) but also how confident we are in that estimate. In this exercise I learnt to plot error bars, confidence intervals and uncertainty using `ggdist`. ## Reflections The difference between standard error bars and confidence intervals was much clearer after seeing them plotted side by side. The 95% CI version (mean ± 1.96 × SE) is more informative than just showing SE. The interactive version combining `plotly` and `DT` was the most practical, being able to hover over a point and see the exact confidence interval values, and then filter the table at the same time, is very useful for presentations. `stat_gradientinterval()` from `ggdist` was visually striking — the gradient effect gives a more intuitive feel for where the uncertainty is concentrated. ## Getting Started ### Loading packages ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pacman::p_load(plotly, crosstalk, DT, ggdist, ggridges, colorspace, gganimate, tidyverse) ``` ::: ### Importing data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam <- read_csv("Exam_data.csv") ``` ::: ### Computing summary statistics ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} my_sum <- exam %>% group_by(RACE) %>% summarise( n=n(), mean=mean(MATHS), sd=sd(MATHS) ) %>% mutate(se=sd/sqrt(n-1)) ``` ::: ## Visualising Uncertainty — ggplot2 methods ### Standard error bars ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex04c_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(my_sum) + geom_errorbar( aes(x=RACE, ymin=mean-se, ymax=mean+se), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9, linewidth=0.5) + geom_point(aes(x=RACE, y=mean), stat="identity", color="red", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + ggtitle("Standard error of mean maths score by race") ``` ::: ::: ### 95% Confidence interval ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex04c_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(my_sum) + geom_errorbar( aes(x=reorder(RACE, -mean), ymin=mean-1.96*se, ymax=mean+1.96*se), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9, linewidth=0.5) + geom_point(aes(x=RACE, y=mean), stat="identity", color="red", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + labs(x = "Maths score", title = "95% confidence interval of mean maths score by race") ``` ::: ::: ### Interactive error bars — 99% CI ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} shared_df = SharedData$new(my_sum) bscols(widths = c(4,8), ggplotly((ggplot(shared_df) + geom_errorbar(aes( x=reorder(RACE, -mean), ymin=mean-2.58*se, ymax=mean+2.58*se), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9, size=0.5) + geom_point(aes( x=RACE, y=mean, text = paste("Race:", `RACE`, "
N:", `n`, "
Avg. Scores:", round(mean, digits = 2), "
95% CI:[", round((mean-2.58*se), digits = 2), ",", round((mean+2.58*se), digits = 2),"]")), stat="identity", color="red", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + xlab("Race") + ylab("Average Scores") + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text( angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + ggtitle("99% Confidence interval of average maths scores by race")), tooltip = "text"), DT::datatable(shared_df, rownames = FALSE, class="compact", width="100%", options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX=T), colnames = c("No. of pupils", "Avg Scores", "Std Dev", "Std Error")) %>% formatRound(columns=c('mean', 'sd', 'se'), digits=2)) ``` ::: ::: ## Visualising Uncertainty — ggdist methods ### stat_pointinterval() ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex04c_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_pointinterval() + labs(title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score", subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ### stat_pointinterval() with .width ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex04c_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_pointinterval(.width = 0.95, .point = median, .interval = qi) + labs(title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score", subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ### stat_gradientinterval() ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex04c_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_gradientinterval( fill = "skyblue", show.legend = TRUE ) + labs(title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score", subtitle = "Gradient + interval plot") ``` ::: :::
